epics.title: Core Parsing Engine
epics.description: Regex-based parsers for all AGENTS.md markdown file formats — release plan, test cases, bugs, cost log, coverage JSON, and progress log.

epics.title: HTML Dashboard Renderer
epics.description: Static HTML generation with six tabs (Hierarchy, Kanban, Traceability, Charts, Costs, Bugs), top bar stats, filters, and recent activity panel.

epics.title: Installation and Distribution
epics.description: One-command bash installer, JSON config system, and Claude Code stop hook for automated cost capture.

epics.title: CI/CD Pipeline
epics.description: Consolidated GitHub Actions pipeline with ESLint, Jest coverage gate, npm audit, CodeQL analysis, GitHub Pages deployment, and Dependabot.

epics.title: Project Self-Documentation
epics.description: Full AGENTS.md-compliant documentation including design doc, architecture, release plan, test cases, ID registry, and all supporting project files.

stories.title: As a developer, I want to parse RELEASE_PLAN.md into structured epics, stories, and tasks, so that the dashboard can visualise project hierarchy.

acs.text: Epics are extracted from fenced code blocks with id, title, status, and releaseTarget fields
Stories are associated with their parent epic via epicId
Acceptance criteria items are parsed with done/undone state

stories.title: As a developer, I want to parse TEST_CASES.md, so that test cases can be linked to stories in the traceability matrix.

acs.text: Test cases are extracted with id, title, relatedStory, relatedAC, type, and status fields
Status is normalised to Pass, Fail, or Not Run from checkbox syntax

stories.title: As a developer, I want to parse BUGS.md, so that bugs are displayed in the Bugs tab and linked to stories.

acs.text: Bugs are extracted with id, title, severity, status, relatedStory, fixBranch, and lessonEncoded fields
Multiple bugs are correctly separated using sliding-window slicing

stories.title: As a developer, I want to parse AI_COST_LOG.md and aggregate costs by branch, so that AI spend can be attributed to user stories.

acs.text: All pipe-delimited table rows are parsed into typed cost objects
Token counts and costs are summed correctly per branch
Sessions on the same branch are accumulated, not overwritten

stories.title: As a developer, I want to parse Jest coverage-summary.json, so that coverage metrics appear in the dashboard top bar.

acs.text: Lines, statements, functions, and branches percentages are extracted
overall is computed as the minimum of all four metrics; meetsTarget is true when overall >= 80

stories.title: As a developer, I want to parse progress.md for recent session summaries, so that the dashboard shows recent activity.

acs.text: Sessions are extracted in reverse-chronological order
The What Was Done section is summarised to 3 lines maximum

stories.title: As a developer, I want to compute projected and actual AI costs per story, so that the Costs tab shows budget vs. spend.

acs.text: Projected cost is computed from t-shirt size × hourly rate
AI cost is attributed to stories by exact branch name match

stories.title: As a developer, I want at-risk stories to be automatically detected, so that I can see which stories need attention without reading every file.

acs.text: Stories with ACs but no linked TCs are flagged missingTCs
In-Progress stories with no branch are flagged noBranch
Stories with a Fail TC and no defect raised are flagged failedTCNoBug

stories.title: As a developer, I want a static HTML dashboard with six tabs, so that I can view all project dimensions in one file.

acs.text: All six tabs (Hierarchy, Kanban, Traceability, Charts, Costs, Bugs) render without error
The top bar shows project name, progress bar, and all six stat tiles
The generated HTML is self-contained and opens in any browser without a server

stories.title: As a developer, I want real-time filters for epic, status, priority, and search, so that I can focus on the subset of stories I care about.

acs.text: Selecting an epic hides stories from other epics in both Hierarchy and Kanban tabs
Free-text search filters by story ID and title

stories.title: As a developer, I want to install PlanVisualizer into my project with a single bash command, so that setup takes under 5 minutes.

acs.text: The install script copies tools/, tests/, and jest.config.js to the target project
The script merges plan:test, plan:test:coverage, and plan:generate scripts into package.json without overwriting existing scripts
The script is idempotent — safe to re-run for updates

stories.title: As a developer, I want Claude Code session costs to be captured automatically, so that AI_COST_LOG.md is always up to date without manual entry.

acs.text: The stop hook appends one row per session to AI_COST_LOG.md using append-safe file open
The header row is written only when the file is empty

stories.title: As a developer, I want to configure all file paths and project metadata in a single JSON file, so that PlanVisualizer works with any project structure.

acs.text: All config keys have sensible defaults — the tool runs with zero config
User-supplied values deep-merge over defaults without clobbering unspecified keys

stories.title: As a developer, I want ESLint to run on every push and PR, so that code quality issues are caught before merge.

acs.text: eslint.config.js uses eslint:recommended with no-eval and eqeqeq as errors
npm run lint exits 0 on all current source files
The lint job in ci.yml fails the workflow if ESLint reports any error

stories.title: As a developer, I want the CI pipeline to gate on 80% coverage, so that test coverage cannot silently regress.

acs.text: jest.config.js defines coverageThreshold with 80% for lines, branches, functions, and statements
npm run test:coverage fails with a descriptive message when any metric falls below 80%
The test job in ci.yml fails the workflow when the threshold is not met

stories.title: As a developer, I want npm audit to run on every push and PR, so that known vulnerabilities in dependencies are caught automatically.

acs.text: The audit job runs npm audit --audit-level=moderate
The workflow fails if any moderate, high, or critical vulnerability is found

stories.title: As a developer, I want CodeQL to analyse the codebase on every PR and push to main, so that security vulnerabilities are caught before they reach production.

acs.text: codeql.yml runs on pull_request, push to main, and a weekly Monday schedule
The security-extended query pack is used for JavaScript analysis
Results are uploaded to the GitHub Security tab as SARIF

stories.title: As a developer, I want Dependabot to open automated PRs for outdated dependencies, so that the project stays secure without manual monitoring.

acs.text: dependabot.yml configures weekly npm updates with a 5-PR limit
GitHub Actions dependencies are also monitored weekly

stories.title: As a contributor, I want a design document and technical architecture reference, so that I can understand the project's goals and implementation without reading all the source code.

acs.text: Docs/DESIGN.md covers product vision, user profile, core concepts, feature set, and design system
Docs/ARCHITECTURE.md covers module structure, data flow, parser contract, renderer design, and CI architecture
Both documents are committed to main

stories.title: As a contributor, I want a complete release plan with epics, stories, tasks, and ID registry, so that project scope and progress can be tracked in the dashboard.

acs.text: Docs/RELEASE_PLAN.md contains all 5 epics with correct AGENTS.md format
All user stories include priority, estimate, status, branch, and acceptance criteria
Docs/ID_REGISTRY.md is populated with correct next-available IDs

stories.title: As a contributor, I want a human-readable test plan and test cases document, so that QA verification is reproducible and stories cannot close without test evidence.

acs.text: Docs/TEST_CASES.md contains at least one TC per user story
Every acceptance criterion (AC) has a corresponding TC
TC format is parseable by parse-test-cases.js and renders in the Traceability tab

stories.title: As a contributor, I want all AGENTS.md-required project files to exist and be populated, so that any AI agent can pick up this project without missing context.

acs.text: MEMORY.md, PROMPT_LOG.md, MIGRATION_LOG.md, findings.md, progress.md, task_plan.md exist and are populated
Docs/LESSONS.md and architecture/ERROR_TAXONOMY.md exist
node tools/generate-plan.js runs successfully and produces a valid plan-status.html

tasks.title: Install eslint@9 and @eslint/js as devDependencies
tasks.notes: npm install --save-dev eslint@9 @eslint/js

tasks.title: Create eslint.config.js with recommended + security rules
tasks.notes: Use flat config format; scope to tools/**/*.js

tasks.title: Add lint script to package.json and verify clean run
tasks.notes: npm run lint must exit 0 on current codebase

tasks.title: Add lint job to ci.yml
tasks.notes: Runs on all branches and PRs

tasks.title: Add coverageThreshold block to jest.config.js
tasks.notes: 80% for lines, branches, functions, statements globally

tasks.title: Verify npm run test:coverage passes threshold
tasks.notes: Current coverage is well above 80% — should pass immediately

tasks.title: Add test job to ci.yml replacing existing test job
tasks.notes: Keep --ci flag for GitHub Actions

tasks.title: Add audit job to ci.yml
tasks.notes: npm audit --audit-level=moderate

tasks.title: Create .github/workflows/codeql.yml
tasks.notes: Triggers: pull_request, push to main, schedule (Monday 08:00 UTC)

tasks.title: Create .github/dependabot.yml
tasks.notes: npm + github-actions ecosystems; weekly; 5-PR limit

tasks.title: Write Docs/DESIGN.md
tasks.notes: Cover vision, user profile, core concepts, features, design system, data flow

tasks.title: Write Docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
tasks.notes: Cover module structure, data flow, parser contract, renderer, CI/CD

tasks.title: Write Docs/RELEASE_PLAN.md with all 5 epics and 22 stories
tasks.notes: Must use AGENTS.md format inside fenced code blocks

tasks.title: Write Docs/ID_REGISTRY.md
tasks.notes: Track next available ID per sequence

tasks.title: Write Docs/TEST_CASES.md with one TC per story AC
tasks.notes: Use TC format parseable by parse-test-cases.js

tasks.title: Create MEMORY.md with project knowledge base
tasks.notes: Organised by topic, not chronologically

tasks.title: Create progress.md, PROMPT_LOG.md, MIGRATION_LOG.md, findings.md, task_plan.md
tasks.notes: Bootstrap initial entries for each file

tasks.title: Create Docs/LESSONS.md and architecture/ERROR_TAXONOMY.md
tasks.notes: Encode Jest upgrade lesson; define error taxonomy

tasks.title: Create plan-visualizer.config.json for this project
tasks.notes: project.name = PlanVisualizer; commit this file (remove from .gitignore for this repo)

tasks.title: Run node tools/generate-plan.js and verify output
tasks.notes: Output should render all tabs with real project data

testCases.title: Parse release plan with two epics and three stories
Parse release plan with empty markdown
Parse release plan acceptance criteria done/undone state
Parse test cases with multiple TCs
Parse test case status normalisation
Parse bugs with multiple entries
Parse AI cost log and aggregate by branch
Attribute AI costs to stories by branch
Parse coverage JSON and compute overall
Detect at-risk story with no linked test cases
Detect at-risk story — In Progress with no branch
Detect at-risk story — failed TC with no bug
Generate full HTML with all data populated
Generate HTML with all source files missing or empty
Filter by epic hides stories from other epics
Free-text search filters by story ID
Install script creates all required files in a fresh target directory
Install script is idempotent
Cost capture hook appends row without overwriting existing rows
Config overrides defaults without clobbering unspecified keys
ESLint passes on all tools/**/*.js files
Jest coverage threshold gate blocks at sub-80% coverage
npm audit passes with zero moderate vulnerabilities

recentActivity.summary: Initial project scaffolding by original author; Implemented all 9 parser modules (tools/lib/*.js); Implemented render-html.js with 6-tab dashboard

tagline: Self-documenting project dashboards for Claude Code.
